course_description:
This course is a supervised project on a research topic in mathematics, approved by the Head of Mathematics. Students are required to select a supervisor and a project topic, in consultation with academic staff. Assessment will include a written thesis, an oral seminar presentation, and a defence of the work carried out on the research topic. Note: Students commencing the year-long project in Semester 1 enrol in MATH6100 for Semester 1 and Semester 2; students commencing the year-long project in Semester 2 enrol in MATH6101 for Semester 2 and the following Semester 1. Students completing their project within one semester enrol in MATH6030 in Semester 1 or Semester 2.

Supervised project on research topic in mathematics.

MATH6100 is for students doing their complete honours thesis in mathematics in two semesters if they are starting their thesis in Semester 1. Students must enrol in the same project code in consecutive semesters.

In the School of Mathematics and Physics we are committed to creating an inclusive and empowering learning environment for all students. We value and respect the diverse range of experiences our students bring to their education, and we believe that this diversity is crucial for fostering a rich culture of knowledge sharing and meaningful exploration. We hold both students and staff accountable for actively contributing to the establishment of a respectful and supportive learning environment.

Bullying, harassment, and discrimination in any form are strictly against our principles and againstﾠ UQ Policy, ﾠand will not be tolerated. We have developed aﾠ suite of resources to assist you in recognising, reporting, and addressing such behaviour. If you have any concerns about your experience in this course, we encourage you to tell a member of the course teaching team, or alternatively contact an SMP Classroom Inclusivity Champion (see Blackboard for contact details). Our Inclusivity Champions are here to listen, to understand your concerns, and to explore potential actions that can be taken to resolve them. Your well-being and a positive learning atmosphere are of utmost importance to us.

restrictions: Enrolment restricted to BSc(Hons) and BAdvSc(Hons) in Mathematics or BMath(Hons).

timetable:
Additional timetable information
Students will usually meet weekly with their advisor.
course_aims: The aim of the honours project in mathematics/statistics is for the student to develop the following skills:

learning_outcomes.description: Be able to plan, develop and carry out an independent research project.
Obtain an in-depth knowledge of the specific topic area through a thorough literature review.
Develop research skills and be able to locate and use information sources.
Present results of research in a written thesis.
Present results of research in an oral seminar.

assessment_details.title: Mid-Year Review
assessment_details.task_description: At the midpoint of their project, students must submit a written literature review and progress report, indicating the progress they have made on their thesis, the material they have consulted, and their plans for the remainder of the semester. The same week, the student will arrange a time with their supervisor(s) + second grader to present and discuss the progress of the project. Read full task description Hide full task description
assessment_details.submission_guidelines:
The literature review and progress report is to be submitted electronically to the supervisor(s), second reader, and honours coordinator.
Deferral or extension
You may be able to apply for an extension .
The maximum extension allowed is 28 days. Extensions are given in multiples of 24 hours.
See ADDITIONAL ASSESSMENT INFORMATION for further extension/deferral information relating to this assessment item.
Late submission
A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.

assessment_details.title: Honours Thesis
assessment_details.task_description: At the end of their second semester of work, each student submits their honours thesis, the final written report of around 20,000-30,000 words in length. Read full task description Hide full task description
assessment_details.submission_guidelines:
The thesis is to be submitted electronically to the supervisor(s), second reader, honours coordinator, and the school office.

assessment_details.title: Honours Project Defence
assessment_details.task_description: During the examination period of their second semester, each student shall attend an oral examination with two assessors (supervisor and examiner) who have read the thesis and attended the seminar. After the oral defence and discussion, the assessors will submit their percentage marks conforming to the standard guidelines for grades of honours.
